metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_5582
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Figure 2: Talin-2 interacts with PIPK (phosphatidylinositol-(4)-phosphate 5-kinase type Iγ).

Figure 3: Microinjection of PIPK pep disrupts the actin cytoskeleton at synapses.

Figure 5: over-expression of human talin 2 head (aa 1-438, GST fusion protein) at lamprey synapses causes ultrastructural changes. (analogous data in Figure 4 for PIPK)

Authors conclude:
"the interaction of PIP kinase with talin in presynaptic compartments provides a mechanism to coordinate PI(4,5)P2 synthesis, actin dynamics, and endocytosis, and further supports a functional link between actin and clathrin-mediated endocytosis.". Experimental description: evidence is shown for both Human and Lamprey Talin-2, we annotated the former;
"Human PIPKIγ tail (aa 635-662) and human talin 2 head (aa 1-438) were expressed as GST fusion proteins and purified using a standard protocol"
